methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $39.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.5B - $14.0B.

private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series C), post-money $4B

data.narrative:
Figure AI, a Series C AI & Software company founded by Brett Adcock, commands a $39.0B post-money valuation after raising $1B+. The relevant peer universe comprises four same-stage, same-sector private companies (Skild AI at $14.0B, Luma AI at $4.0B, Runway and Magic AI each at $1.5B) alongside one public comparable, Palantir.

Two valuation methods apply here. Stage-progression analysis, using a standard 1.7x multiplier for Series C advancement, implies $66.3B. Peer-median analysis across the four identified comparables yields $2.8B, with a range spanning $1.5B to $14.0B. The stark disparity reflects the heterogeneous positioning within the AI & Software cohort; Skild AI's $14.0B valuation anchors the upper bound, while three peers cluster at or below $4.0B.

The triangulated analysis yields a wide range: $2.3B (low), $34.5B (median), and $76.2B (high). The $39.0B current valuation aligns closely with the median output, sitting within the interquartile spread. Key uncertainties include the composition and maturity profile of the peer set—the four-company sample is modest—and whether stage-progression assumptions, calibrated on typical venture outcomes, apply to an outlier-valued Series C. The 3.3x spread between low and high estimates underscores sensitivity to methodology selection and peer comparability assumptions.
